moduleDescription: This module implements a low-level cross-platform sockets interface. Look at the net module for the higher-level version.

entries.name: Port
entries.code: Port = distinct uint16
entries.description: port type

entries.name: Domain
entries.code:
Domain = enum
  AF_UNSPEC = 0, ## unspecified domain (can be detected automatically by
                  ## some procedures, such as getaddrinfo)
  AF_UNIX = 1,              ## for local socket (using a file). Unsupported on Windows.
  AF_INET = 2,              ## for network protocol IPv4 or
  AF_INET6 = 30
entries.description: domain, which specifies the protocol family of the created socket. Other domains than those that are listed here are unsupported.

entries.name: SockType
entries.code:
SockType = enum
  SOCK_STREAM = 1,          ## reliable stream-oriented service or Stream Sockets
  SOCK_DGRAM = 2,           ## datagram service or Datagram Sockets
  SOCK_RAW = 3,             ## raw protocols atop the network layer.
  SOCK_SEQPACKET = 5         ## reliable sequenced packet service
entries.description: second argument to socket proc

entries.name: Protocol
entries.code:
Protocol = enum
  IPPROTO_TCP = 6,          ## Transmission control protocol.
  IPPROTO_UDP = 17,         ## User datagram protocol.
  IPPROTO_IP,               ## Internet protocol.
  IPPROTO_IPV6,             ## Internet Protocol Version 6.
  IPPROTO_RAW,              ## Raw IP Packets Protocol. Unsupported on Windows.
  IPPROTO_ICMP,             ## Internet Control message protocol.
  IPPROTO_ICMPV6             ## Internet Control message protocol for IPv6.
entries.description: third argument to socket proc

entries.name: Servent
entries.code:
Servent = object
  name*: string
  aliases*: seq[string]
  port*: Port
  proto*: string
entries.description: information about a service

entries.name: Hostent
entries.code:
Hostent = object
  name*: string
  aliases*: seq[string]
  addrtype*: Domain
  length*: int
  addrList*: seq[string]
entries.description: information about a given host

entries.name: IPPROTO_NONE
entries.code: IPPROTO_NONE = IPPROTO_IP
entries.description: Use this if your socket type requires a protocol value of zero (e.g. Unix sockets).

entries.name: osInvalidSocket
entries.code: osInvalidSocket = INVALID_SOCKET

entries.name: IOCPARM_MASK
entries.code: IOCPARM_MASK = 127

entries.name: ioctlsocket

entries.code: proc `==`(a, b: Port): bool {.borrow, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: == for ports.

entries.code: proc `$`(p: Port): string {.borrow, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the port number as a string

entries.name: toInt
entries.code: proc toInt(domain: Domain): cint {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts the Domain enum to a platform-dependent cint.

entries.name: toInt
entries.code: proc toInt(typ: SockType): cint {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts the SockType enum to a platform-dependent cint.

entries.name: toInt
entries.code: proc toInt(p: Protocol): cint {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts the Protocol enum to a platform-dependent cint.

entries.name: toKnownDomain
entries.code:
proc toKnownDomain(family: cint): Option[Domain] {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts the platform-dependent cint to the Domain or none(), if the cint is not known.

entries.name: toSockType
entries.code:
proc toSockType(protocol: Protocol): SockType {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: getProtoByName
entries.code:
proc getProtoByName(name: string): int {.raises: [OSError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a protocol code from the database that matches the protocol name.

entries.name: close
entries.code: proc close(socket: SocketHandle) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Closes a socket.

entries.name: setInheritable
entries.code:
proc setInheritable(s: SocketHandle; inheritable: bool): bool {.inline,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Set whether a socket is inheritable by child processes. Returns true on success.

This function is not implemented on all platform, test for availability with declared() <system.html#declared,untyped>.

entries.name: createNativeSocket
entries.description:
Creates a new socket; returns osInvalidSocket if an error occurs.

inheritable decides if the resulting SocketHandle can be inherited by child processes.

Use this overload if one of the enums specified above does not contain what you need.

entries.name: createNativeSocket
entries.description:
Creates a new socket; returns osInvalidSocket if an error occurs.

inheritable decides if the resulting SocketHandle can be inherited by child processes.

entries.name: bindAddr
entries.code:
proc bindAddr(socket: SocketHandle; name: ptr SockAddr; namelen: SockLen): cint {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: listen
entries.code:
proc listen(socket: SocketHandle; backlog = SOMAXCONN): cint {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Marks socket as accepting connections. Backlog specifies the maximum length of the queue of pending connections.

entries.name: getAddrInfo
entries.code:
proc getAddrInfo(address: string; port: Port; hints: AddrInfo): ptr AddrInfo {.
    raises: [OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Warning: The resulting ptr AddrInfo must be freed using freeAddrInfo!

entries.name: getAddrInfo
entries.description: Warning: The resulting ptr AddrInfo must be freed using freeAddrInfo!

entries.name: ntohl
entries.code: proc ntohl(x: uint32): uint32 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts 32-bit unsigned integers from network to host byte order. On machines where the host byte order is the same as network byte order, this is a no-op; otherwise, it performs a 4-byte swap operation.

entries.name: ntohs
entries.code: proc ntohs(x: uint16): uint16 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts 16-bit unsigned integers from network to host byte order. On machines where the host byte order is the same as network byte order, this is a no-op; otherwise, it performs a 2-byte swap operation.

entries.name: htonl
entries.code: template htonl(x: uint32): untyped
entries.description: Converts 32-bit unsigned integers from host to network byte order. On machines where the host byte order is the same as network byte order, this is a no-op; otherwise, it performs a 4-byte swap operation.

entries.name: htons
entries.code: template htons(x: uint16): untyped
entries.description: Converts 16-bit unsigned integers from host to network byte order. On machines where the host byte order is the same as network byte order, this is a no-op; otherwise, it performs a 2-byte swap operation.

entries.name: getSockDomain
entries.code:
proc getSockDomain(socket: SocketHandle): Domain {.raises: [OSError, IOError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the socket's domain (AF_INET or AF_INET6).

entries.name: getServByName
entries.code:
proc getServByName(name, proto: string): Servent {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [OSError], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Searches the database from the beginning and finds the first entry for which the service name specified by name matches the s_name member and the protocol name specified by proto matches the s_proto member.

On posix this will search through the /etc/services file.

entries.name: getServByPort
entries.code:
proc getServByPort(port: Port; proto: string): Servent {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [OSError], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Searches the database from the beginning and finds the first entry for which the port specified by port matches the s_port member and the protocol name specified by proto matches the s_proto member.

On posix this will search through the /etc/services file.

entries.name: getHostByAddr
entries.code:
proc getHostByAddr(ip: string): Hostent {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [OSError, IOError, Exception], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This function will lookup the hostname of an IP Address.

entries.name: getHostByName
entries.code:
proc getHostByName(name: string): Hostent {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [OSError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This function will lookup the IP address of a hostname.

entries.name: getHostname
entries.code:
proc getHostname(): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [OSError],
                             forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the local hostname (not the FQDN)

entries.name: getAddrString
entries.code:
proc getAddrString(sockAddr: ptr SockAddr): string {.
    raises: [Exception, OSError, IOError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the string representation of address within sockAddr

entries.name: getAddrString
entries.code:
proc getAddrString(sockAddr: ptr SockAddr; strAddress: var string) {.
    raises: [Exception, OSError, IOError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Stores in strAddress the string representation of the address inside sockAddr

Note

  • strAddress must be initialized to 46 in length.

entries.name: getSockName
entries.code:
proc getSockName(socket: SocketHandle): Port {.raises: [OSError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the socket's associated port number.

entries.name: getLocalAddr
entries.code:
proc getLocalAddr(socket: SocketHandle; domain: Domain): (string, Port) {.
    raises: [OSError, Exception], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the socket's local address and port number.

Similar to POSIX's getsockname.

entries.name: getPeerAddr
entries.code:
proc getPeerAddr(socket: SocketHandle; domain: Domain): (string, Port) {.
    raises: [OSError, Exception], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the socket's peer address and port number.

Similar to POSIX's getpeername

entries.name: getSockOptInt
entries.code:
proc getSockOptInt(socket: SocketHandle; level, optname: int): int {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [OSError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: getsockopt for integer options.

entries.name: setSockOptInt
entries.code:
proc setSockOptInt(socket: SocketHandle; level, optname, optval: int) {.
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], raises: [OSError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: setsockopt for integer options.

entries.name: setBlocking
entries.code:
proc setBlocking(s: SocketHandle; blocking: bool) {.raises: [OSError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Sets blocking mode on socket.

Raises OSError on error.

entries.name: selectRead
entries.code:
proc selectRead(readfds: var seq[SocketHandle]; timeout = 500): int {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
When a socket in readfds is ready to be read from then a non-zero value will be returned specifying the count of the sockets which can be read from. The sockets which cannot be read from will also be removed from readfds.

timeout is specified in milliseconds and -1 can be specified for an unlimited time.

entries.name: selectWrite
entries.code:
proc selectWrite(writefds: var seq[SocketHandle]; timeout = 500): int {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
When a socket in writefds is ready to be written to then a non-zero value will be returned specifying the count of the sockets which can be written to. The sockets which cannot be written to will also be removed from writefds.

timeout is specified in milliseconds and -1 can be specified for an unlimited time.

entries.name: accept
entries.code:
proc accept(fd: SocketHandle; inheritable = defined(nimInheritHandles)): (
    SocketHandle, string) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Accepts a new client connection.

inheritable decides if the resulting SocketHandle can be inherited by child processes.

Returns (osInvalidSocket, "") if an error occurred.
